summary: DHH advocates for releasing technical books in beta digital form rather than waiting months for traditional publishing processes to complete. He describes pushing Dave Thomas to release 'Agile Web Development with Rails' as a digital beta while the 500+ page manuscript undergoes editing, typesetting, and printing. The post argues that the traditional publishing timeline is bad for both users who need documentation now and publishers who want to sell it. He acknowledges publisher concerns about piracy and perception of unfinished work but dismisses them, betting on the Rails community's goodwill. The core proposal applies the open source 'release early, release often' philosophy to book publishing.
key_points: The traditional publishing pipeline creates an artificial months-long gap between content completion and availability, harming both readers and publishers
A 90% finished book delivered now is far more valuable than a perfect book delivered in two and a half months
The open source development model of releasing early and iterating can and should be applied to commercial documentation
Publisher fears about piracy and diminished interest in the final product are overblown, especially in passionate technical communities
The demand for Rails documentation far outpaced the publishing industry's ability to respond
money_quotes: When you're used to the web, the book publishing business can appear utterly arcane.

Instead of letting a ton of high quality, really important documentation sit on the shelf for two and a half months while the machinery of publishing is ready to deliver, we'll be trying to get the book out in digital form for those that really want it.
Especially so when the choice is between no book and a 90% done book.
Show publishers that we want the quality writers that they all have lured in to start delivering according to the open source model.
There are so many interesting topics that could use a flow of high quality, commercially-backed documentation production today. Not 6 months from now.
Once the publishers recognize there's a market, it easily takes 6 months (or longer!) before the final book is ready in the stores.
key_insight: Technical book publishing should adopt the open source model of releasing early and iterating, because a beta book today is more valuable than a perfect book months from now.
